aperoInfo.tradition: Raise your glass, say "À la vôtre!" or "Santé!".
Raise your glass and say "Cheers!" or "Ayekoo!" — a Ghanaian expression of appreciation and celebration.
Say "Letenachin!" (To our health!) while clinking glasses — Ethiopian coffee ceremony is as important as the beer culture.
Raise your glass, say "À votre santé!" or "بالصحة والعافية!" while making eye contact with those around you. It's a warm and hospitable Algerian toast.
Raise your glass and say "ንጥዓናና!" (To our health!). Eritrean café culture in Asmara has an Italian flair from colonial times — espresso alongside beer.
Raise your glass, say "À la vôtre!", "Santé!" or "A bè san dèmè".
Raise your glass, say "Santé!" and enjoy the evening breeze along the Ubangi River.
Toast with "Cheers!" and sip cold Julbrew at a beach bar — The Gambia's tourism scene has a vibrant sunset drink tradition.
Say "Saúde!" while drinking cashew wine — Guinea-Bissau is one of Africa's largest cashew producers and the fermented cashew drink is a local pride.
Say "Cheers!" or "Zikomo!" (Thank you/Cheers in Chichewa) by Lake Malawi as the sun sets over the water.
Raise your glass, say "À votre santé!", "Eza biongo!" or "Bolingo ya vie".
Say "Amahoro!" (Peace!) while clinking glasses at a lakeside bar overlooking Lake Tanganyika.
Raise your glass, say "Cheers!`", "في صحتك!", "صحة وسلامة!".
Raise your glass, say "Bisaha!", "على صحتك!" or "Saha wa afla!".
Say "¡Salud!" — Ceuta blends Spanish tapas culture with North African flavours, making its apéro scene uniquely Mediterranean.
Say "Santé!" by the Atlantic coast as the sun sets over the Konakry peninsula.
Raise your glass, say "À la vôtre!", "Santé!" or "Na nga def!".
Say "Afya!" (Health!) — Dar es Salaam's beach bars and rooftop terraces make for one of East Africa's finest sundowner experiences.
Say "Santé!" in this crossroads of cultures — Djibouti blends French colonial café culture with East African and Middle Eastern flavours.
Say "Santé!" or "Chamba!" — Douala's lively bar scene along the Wouri estuary is the economic heartbeat of Central Africa.
Say "Bisaha!" over mint tea in a nomadic tent — the Saharan apéro is built around Atay tea ceremonies, shared slowly over three rounds.
Say "Na we time!" (It's our time!) — Freetown's beach bars on the Lumley Beach strip host one of West Africa's most relaxed sundowner scenes.
Say "Pula!" (Rain/Blessing!) — Botswana's sundowner tradition echoes Southern Africa's bush culture: drinks at the golden hour, often with wildlife in view.
Say "Cheers!" at a kopje (rocky hillside) sundowner — Zimbabwe's colonial-era club and safari culture made the 17:30 gin & tonic a beloved institution.

countryInfo.name: South Africa
countryInfo.language: Afrikaans, English, Zulu, Xhosa, and more

aperoInfo.tradition: Raise your glass, say "Cheers!", "Gesondheid!" or "Iphumile lezinto!".
Say "Cheers!" along the Nile riverbank — South Sudan's apéro scene is young but vibrant, often accompanied by traditional Dinka music.
Say "Webale!" (Thank you/Cheers in Luganda) — Kampala's rooftop bars with views of the seven hills are the city's favourite apéro spots.
Say "في صحتك!" (To your health!) over a glass of Karkade — Sudan's apéro is largely non-alcoholic, centred on vibrant, sweetened hibiscus and tamarind drinks.
Say "Murakoze!" (Thank you/Cheers!) — Kigali's immaculate hilltop terraces make it one of Africa's most pleasant apéro cities.
Raise your glass, say "À la vôtre!", "Na lufelo!" or "Na mapon'ezoba!".
Shout "Cheers!" or "E go better!" — Lagos's beach bars on Victoria Island and Lekki Phase 1 are West Africa's most electric apéro destinations.
Say "Santé!" over a cold Régab — Libreville's equatorial coast has a relaxed French-African apéro vibe with Atlantic breezes.
Say "Santé!" at a maquis (open-air bar) along the Atlantic coast — Lomé's beach culture makes for a lively and affordable apéro scene.
Say "Saúde!" (Health!) — Luanda's bay-view restaurants and petroleum-boom rooftop bars mix Portuguese café culture with Angolan energy.

snacks: Poulet à la Moambé (Palm Nut Chicken)
aperoInfo.tradition: Say "Mbote!" (Hello/Cheers in Lingala) — Lubumbashi's copper-boom terraces host a lively apéro culture under the Southern African sky.
Say "Twalumba!" (We are grateful!) — Zambia's sundowner tradition echoes Southern Africa's bush camp culture at 17:30 sharp.
Say "¡Salud!" or "Santé!" — Malabo's unique blend of Spanish, French and African cultures creates a cosmopolitan island apéro vibe.
Say "Saúde!" over piri piri prawns and a 2M Beer — Maputo's bay-view restaurants are some of Southern Africa's most underrated apéro destinations.
Say "Khotso!" (Peace!) — Lesotho's highland sundowners have a crisp mountain air quality that makes the first sip particularly rewarding.
Say "Siyabonga!" (Thank you/Cheers!) — Eswatini's royal Buganu (marula wine) festival in February is the country's most festive drinking tradition.
Say "Caafimaad!" (Health!) — Somalia's apéro is entirely non-alcoholic, built around aromatic Shaah tea and communal sharing of food in the cool evening air.
Say "Cheers!" — Liberia's American-influenced English-speaking culture meets West African tradition at Monrovia's beach bars on the Atlantic.
Raise your glass, say "Cheers!", "Afya yako!" or "Mazuri!".
Say "Santé!" over a cold Gala Beer — Chad's Sahel climate means the evening hours are the most social, with drinks in the cooling shade.
Say "Santé!" as the Sahara sun sets over the Niger River — Niamey's riverside bars make for a surprisingly pleasant desert apéro.
Say "في صحتك!" over Atay tea — Mauritania is predominantly non-alcoholic; the three-round Atay tea ceremony is the apéro, served in tiny glasses with an ocean of hospitality.
Raise your glass, say "À la vôtre!", "A la santé!" or "Laafi bala!".
Say "Santé!" over a glass of Sodabi — Benin's palm spirit is as integral to social gatherings as wine is in France.
Say "Saúde!" over cold Rosema Beer — São Tomé's cocoa island setting, colonial Portuguese architecture and Atlantic breezes make it one of Africa's most idyllic apéro spots.

toast: في صحتك! (Fi sahtek, meaning 'To your health!')
صحة وعافية! (Sahha wa 'afiya, meaning 'Health and well-being!')
بالحياة والسلام! (Bil-hayat wa's-salam, meaning 'To life and peace!')
aperoInfo.tradition: Raise your glass, say 'في صحتك!' (Fi sahtek), 'صحة وعافية!' (Sahha wa 'afiya) or 'بالحياة والسلام!' (Bil-hayat wa's-salam), and enjoy the flavors of Libya's cuisine and the warmth of its culture.

brands: Thé à la Menthe by El Alj
aperoInfo.tradition: Raise your glass, say "À la vôtre!", "بالصحة والعافية!" or "Choukran!".
Say "Prost!" — Namibia's German colonial heritage means braai (BBQ) culture and cold Windhoek Lager at 17:30 is as sacred as anywhere in the Southern African sundowner belt.
Raise your glass, say "Cheers!". It's about celebrating together.
Raise your glass and say "¡Salud!" or "¡Por la amistad!" while making eye contact with those around you. It's a warm and friendly Argentine toast.
Say "¡Salud!" while passing Tereré — Paraguay's cold mate drink is the social glue of daily life, shared communally with a bombilla straw, especially in the heat of the afternoon.
Say "Cheers!" — in Barbados, socialising is called "liming" and it happens at any hour. Mount Gay, the world's oldest rum distillery (1703), makes every apéro historically significant.
Raise your glass and say "Salud!" or "¡A tu salud!" while making eye contact with those around you. It's a warm and friendly Colombian toast.
Shout "¡Arriba, abajo, al centro, pa'dentro!" — Venezuela's elaborate toast raises the glass up, down, to the centre, then drinks. Tequeños (warm cheese sticks) are the non-negotiable apéro snack.
Raise your glass, say "Cheers!", "To your health!" or "Bottoms up!".f
Say "¡Pura vida!" — Costa Rica's national phrase meaning "pure life" doubles as a perfect toast. Guaro Sour with lime is the quintessential Costa Rican apéro drink.
Raise your glass, say "Cheers!", "To good times!" or "Here's to Denver!".
Raise your glass, say "Cheers!", "To Detroit!" or "Drink up!".
Say "Saúde!" on Iracema Beach as the sun hits the Atlantic — Fortaleza's beach bars serve icy Caipirinha at the most spectacular sunset in Northeast Brazil.
Raise your glass, say "À la vôtre!" or "Santé!".
Say "¡Salud!" — Guatemala's Ron Zacapa is internationally acclaimed as one of the world's finest rums, aged in the mountains at 2,300m altitude.
Say "Cheers!" at a Halifax waterfront pub — Nova Scotia's craft beer scene and the world-famous Donair make for the Maritime provinces' finest apéro.
Say "¡Salud!" at Hemingway's table at La Bodeguita del Medio — Cuba invented the Mojito and the Daiquiri, making every apéro a piece of cocktail history.
Raise your glass, say "Cheers!", "To Indy!" or "Let's raise a glass!".
Raise your glass, say "Cheers!", "To Jamaica!" or "One love!".
Raise your glass, say "Salud!", "Arriba Bolivia!" or "Viva La Paz!".

snacks: Papa a la Huancaina (Potatoes in Spicy Cheese Sauce)
aperoInfo.tradition: Raise your glass, say "Salud!", "Arriba Peru!" or "Viva Lima!".
Raise your glass, say "Cheers!", "To LA!" or "Let's raise a glass!".
Raise your glass, say "Salud!", "¡A tu salud!" or "¡Viva México!".
Raise your glass, say "Cheers!", "To the Big Apple!" or "Drink up!".
Raise your glass, say "Salud!", "¡Por Puerto Rico!" or "Brindemos!", and follow it with a heartfelt smile and warm eye contact. It's a gesture of celebration and unity.
Say "Saúde!" on the Porto de Galinhas beach — Recife's beachside apéro is one of Brazil's most colourful, mixing Carnaval energy with Atlantic breezes year-round.
Say "¡Salud!" — Chile and Peru share the Pisco Sour as their signature apéro drink (each claims ownership). Chilean Carménère wine, a grape nearly extinct in France, is the proud local alternative.
Say "¡Salud!" with a Mamajuana — the DR's herbal rum-wine blend soaked in tree bark and herbs is one of the Caribbean's most distinctive and storied apéro drinks.
When raising your glass for a toast, it's customary to say "Saúde!", "À São Paulo!" or "Vamos brindar!" with enthusiasm.
Say "¡Salud!" — Honduras's cold Salva Vida Beer paired with Baleadas is the definitive Central American apéro combination.
When making a toast, simply say "Cheers!", "To Toronto!" or "Let's raise a glass!" with a friendly smile.
When making a toast, it's common to say "Cheers!", "To Vancouver!" or "Raise your glass!" with a friendly nod.
Say "Cheers!" — Winnipeg's bitterly cold winters make its pub culture all the warmer. Perogies (a Ukranian-Canadian staple) with rye whisky is the prairie apéro classic.
Say "İshіңіz!" (Drink!) or "За здоровье!" (To health!) — Kumiss, the fermented mare's milk, is Kazakhstan's most ancient and ceremonially significant drink, offered to honoured guests.
Say "صحتين!" (Two healths!) — Jordan's mezze culture means the apéro IS the meal. Arak mixed with water and ice (turns milky white) is the Levant's classic apéro spirit.
Say "Saglygynyz üçin!" (To your health!) — Ashgabat's white marble city is one of the most unusual capitals on earth; its apéro scene is quiet but warm with Central Asian hospitality.
Say "صحتين!" (Two healths!) — Iraq's Arak tradition dates back millennia. Masgouf, Baghdad's iconic grilled carp on the Tigris riverbanks, is the ultimate apéro feast.
In Bahrain, when making a toast, say "في صحتك", "لبحرين" or "لنرفع أكوابنا ". It's a gesture of hospitality and camaraderie.
In Baku, when making a toast, say "Sağlığınıza!", "Azərbaycana!" or "Şərab atın!". It's a gesture of warmth and hospitality.

toast: ไปที่กรุงเทพฯ
ขอให้โชคดี
aperoInfo.tradition: In Bangkok, when making a toast, you can say "ชาวโลกสวย", "ไปที่กรุงเทพฯ" or "ขอให้โชคดี". It's a gesture of goodwill and friendship.
In Beirut, when making a toast, say "صحتين", "لبيروت " or "نرفع أكوابنا ". It's a gesture of hospitality and celebration.
Say "Ичели!" (Let's drink!) — Kumiss is Kyrgyzstan's sacred nomadic drink, and sharing it from a communal bowl is a profound act of hospitality in the mountains.
Say "Selamat!" (Congratulations/Cheers!) — Brunei is a dry country (alcohol banned for Muslims), so the apéro is built around elaborate non-alcoholic drinks and rich Malay street food.
Say "Āyubōvan!" (Long life!) — Sri Lanka's coconut Arrack is one of Asia's most distinctive spirits. Sipping it ocean-side in Galle's Dutch fort at sunset is a perfect apéro.
Say "صحتين!" — Damascus, one of the world's oldest continuously inhabited cities, has an apéro tradition that stretches back to ancient Phoenician wine culture. Arak with mezze is timeless.
Say "Shubo Kamona!" (Good wishes!) — Bangladesh is predominantly non-alcoholic; the apéro is built around the ritual of afternoon chai, street snacks and lively bazaar conversation.
When making a toast, say "صحتين ", "لدبي " or "نرفع أكوابنا ". It's a gesture of warm hospitality and togetherness.
Say "Саломатӣ!" (Health!) — Tajikistan's apéro is centred around green tea and communal shashlik grilling, with a glass of vodka reserved for special occasions.
When making a toast, say "صحتين ", "لغزة " or "فلنرفع أكوابنا ". It's a gesture of hospitality and unity, often accompanied by warm smiles.
Shout "Một, hai, ba — Dô!" (One, two, three — Cheers!) — Vietnam's communal low-plastic-stool street beer culture is arguably the world's most democratic apéro: cheap, abundant and sociable.
When making a toast, say "乾杯", "香港加油" or "讓我們乾杯". It's a gesture of enthusiasm and unity.
In Jakarta, when making a toast, say "Selamat!", "Untuk Jakarta!" or "Mari kita angkat gelas!". It's a gesture of warm hospitality and togetherness, often accompanied by laughter.
When making a toast, say "לחיים ", "לירושלים " or "ברוך הבא ". It's a gesture of celebration and unity.
When making a toast, say "صحت ", "برای کابل " or "به صحرا ". It's a gesture of hospitality and camaraderie.
Say "Shukriya!" (Thank you!) — Pakistan is predominantly non-alcoholic; the apéro is a vibrant street food and sharbat culture, most alive in Karachi's Burns Road food street at dusk.
Wen making a toast, say "चियर्स", "काठमाडौंको लागि" or "हाम्रो साथ". It's a gesture of warmth and togetherness, often accompanied by a friendly smile.
Say "Cheers!" at a Kolkata adda (informal gathering) — the city's intellectual café culture and street-food apéro scene are inseparable. Old Monk rum is Bengal's most beloved spirit.
In Kuala Lumpur, when making a toast, say "Sihat!", "Untuk Kuala Lumpur!" or "Mari kita angkat gelas!". It's a gesture of warmth and friendship, often accompanied by laughter.
When making a toast, say "في صحتك ", "للكويت " or "لنرفع أكوابنا ". It's a gesture of hospitality and unity, often accompanied by a warm smile.
Say "Selamat!" (Congratulations!) — Makassar's spice trade heritage and seafood culture make for a rich Sulawesi apéro, often enjoyed watching ships in the historic Losari Beach area.

Say "Mabuhay!" (Long live!) — the Philippines is one of Asia's biggest beer-drinking nations. San Miguel, founded in 1890, is the oldest brewery in Southeast Asia and the unofficial king of Filipino apéro.

Say "صحتين!" over Omani qahwa and dates — Oman's legendary hospitality means guests are always welcomed with this cardamom-spiced coffee and the country's prized dates. A non-alcoholic apéro of extraordinary refinement.
Say "Yiamas!" (Health!) — Cyprus invented Commandaria, the world's oldest named wine still in production (since 800 BC). Halloumi with a cold Keo beer is the island's essential apéro.
Say "Choul Muoy!" (Cheers!) — Phnom Penh's riverfront is one of Southeast Asia's finest apéro settings, where Mekong and Tonlé Sap rivers meet at the golden hour.
When making a toast, say "في صحتك ", "للدوحة " or "لنرفع أكوابنا ". It's a gesture of hospitality and unity, often accompanied by a warm greeting.

snacks: Kabsa (Spiced Rice — often the apéro precedes it)
aperoInfo.tradition: Say "Bismillah!" (In the name of God) before eating — Saudi Arabia is dry (no alcohol). The elaborate date-and-qahwa ritual is the sacred apéro, offered to every guest as the highest form of hospitality.
When making a toast, say "건배", "서울을 위하여" or "잔을 높여요". It's a gesture of camaraderie and celebration, often followed by lively conversations.
When making a toast, say "干杯", "为上海" or "让我们举杯". It's a gesture of camaraderie and celebration, often accompanied by laughter.
When making a toast, say "Cheers!" for "Cheers!", "To Singapore!" or "Let's raise our glasses!". It's a gesture of multicultural unity, often accompanied by a friendly atmosphere.
When making a toast, say "乾杯", "為台北" or "讓我們舉杯". It's a gesture of celebration and unity, often accompanied by a friendly atmosphere.

Say "Sog'ligingiz uchun!" (To your health!) — Uzbekistan's Silk Road cities (Samarkand, Bukhara) have a teahouse culture that is the apéro: sweet green tea, dried fruit and non flatbread in a chaikhana (tea house).

Say "Gaumarjos!" (To victory!) — Georgia is the world's oldest wine region (8,000 years). The tamada (toastmaster) role at Georgian supras (feasts) is a revered art, with elaborate multi-course toasts for each glass.
When making a toast, say "به سلامتی ", "برای تهران " or "بیایید لیوانمان را بالا ببریم ". It's a gesture of warmth and hospitality, often accompanied by a smile.

toast: さあ、杯を上げましょう
aperoInfo.tradition: When making a toast, say "乾杯", "東京に乾杯" or "さあ、杯を上げましょう". It's a gesture of camaraderie and appreciation for good company, often accompanied by a smile.
Say "Эрүүл мэндийн төлөө!" (To health!) — Airag, Mongolia's sacred fermented mare's milk, is offered to honoured guests in a special bowl. Refusing it is considered deeply impolite.
Say "Nyindii Ton Hap!" (Happy to receive you!) — BeerLao is internationally awarded and a point of national pride. Vientiane's Mekong riverside sundowner strip is the most tranquil in Southeast Asia.
Say "Aung myin par say!" (May you succeed!) — Myanmar Beer with Laphet Thoke (pickled tea salad) is the quintessential Yangon apéro. The fermented tea leaf is both a snack and a cultural institution.

Say "Կենաց!" (Kenats! — To life!) — Armenian brandy is Churchill's favourite spirit (he received cases from Stalin at Yalta). Khorovats BBQ with Areni wine at Mount Ararat sunset is the quintessential Armenian apéro.

brands: Adega Cooperativa de São Roque do Pico
aperoInfo.tradition: When making a toast, say "Saúde!", "Às Azores!" or "Vamos brindar!". It's a gesture of warmth and camaraderie, often accompanied by a friendly atmosphere.
When making a toast, say "Cheers!", "To Bermuda!" or "Let's raise our glasses!". It's a gesture of celebration and unity.
When making a toast, say "¡Salud!", "¡A las Islas Canarias!" or "¡Brindemos!". It's a gesture of warmth and celebration.
When making a toast, say "Saúde!", "À Cabo Verde!" or "Vamos brindar!". It's a gesture of celebration and unity.
When making a toast, say "Skál!", "Á Íslandi!" or "Höfum á!". It's a gesture of warmth and celebration.
When making a toast, say "Cheers!", "To Adelaide!" or "Let's raise our glasses!". It's a gesture of camaraderie and celebration, often accompanied by a friendly atmosphere.
When making a toast, say "Cheers!", "To Brisbane!" or "Let's raise our glasses!". It's a gesture of camaraderie and celebration, often accompanied by a friendly atmosphere.

Say "Cheers!" — Darwin's iconic Darwin Stubby (a 2.25-litre bottle of beer) was invented here as the ultimate tropical apéro vessel. Watching lightning storms over the Timor Sea at sunset with a cold stubby is a Top End rite of passage.

Say "Cheers!" — Tasmania produces Australia's finest single malt whisky (Sullivan's Cove won World's Best), freshest oysters and most celebrated pinot noir. An apéro in Hobart's waterfront salamanca district is a gourmet pilgrimage.
When making a toast, say "Cheers!", "To Melbourne!" or "Let's raise our glasses!". It's a gesture of camaraderie and celebration, often accompanied by a friendly atmosphere.

Say "Cheers!" on Cottesloe Beach — Perth's isolation from the rest of Australia has cultivated one of the country's most self-sufficient food and drink cultures. Margaret River wines and Fremantle craft beers at a beach sunset are world-class.
When making a toast, say "Cheers!", "To Sydney!" or "Let's raise our glasses!". It's a gesture of camaraderie and celebration, often accompanied by a friendly atmosphere.
When making a toast, say "Proost!", "Op Amsterdam!" or "Laten we proosten!". It's a gesture of conviviality and celebration, often accompanied by a lively atmosphere.
Say "Salut!" — Andorra's duty-free status makes it a paradise for spirits. The tiny principality's mountain air, ski culture and Catalan apéro tradition combine into something uniquely Pyrenean.
Raise your glass, say "Στην υγειά σας!", and make eye contact with those you are toasting with. It's a warm and heartfelt wish for good health and happiness.
When making a toast, say "Živeli!", "Na zdravlje!" or "Podignimo čaše!". The tradition often involves heartfelt toasts and a strong sense of camaraderie.
Raise your glass, make eye contact, and say "Prost!". It's common to make a firm, audible clink when toasting.
When making a toast, say "Na zdravie!", "Za Bratislavu!" or "Dvihajme poháre!". The tradition often involves heartfelt toasts and a warm sense of camaraderie.
Raise your glass, say "Santé!" or "Proost!", and make eye contact with fellow revelers. It's a gesture of camaraderie.
When making a toast, say "Noroc!", "Pentru București!" or "Să ridicăm paharele!". The tradition often involves heartfelt toasts and a warm sense of camaraderie.
When making a toast, say "Egészségére!", "Budapestért!" or "Emeljük a poharakat!". The tradition often involves heartfelt toasts and a warm sense of camaraderie.
Say "Sănătate!" (Health!) — Moldova houses the world's largest wine cellar (Mileștii Mici, 200km of tunnels). Divin brandy and plăcintă at a village courtyard is the Moldovan apéro at its finest.
When making a toast, say "Skål!", "For København!" or "Lad os hæve vores glas!". The tradition often involves heartfelt toasts and a warm sense of camaraderie.
Raise your glass, look each person in the eye, and say "Sláinte!". Maintain eye contact during the toast.
Say "Cheers!" — Gibraltar's British pub culture meets Spanish tapas on the Rock. The panoramic view of Africa across the Strait makes for one of Europe's most dramatic apéro backdrops.
When making a toast, say "Kippis!", "Helsingille!" or "Nostetaan maljat!". The tradition often involves heartfelt toasts and a warm sense of camaraderie.
When making a toast, say "Şerefe!", "İstanbul için!" or "Kadehlerimizi kaldıralım!". The tradition often involves heartfelt toasts and a warm sense of camaraderie.
Raise your glass, say "Za zdorov'ya!", and maintain eye contact with fellow drinkers to show respect and sincerity.
When making a toast, say 'Saúde!', 'Por Lisboa!' or 'Levantemos os copos!'. The tradition often involves heartfelt toasts and a warm sense of camaraderie.
Say "Na zdravje!" (To health!) — Ljubljana's car-free riverside Promenade lined with café terraces is one of Central Europe's most charming apéro settings. Slivovitz with sausage is the classic.
Raise your glass and say "Cheers!" while making eye contact with those around you. It's a friendly and informal way to toast.
When making a toast, say "Zum Wohl!", "Fir Lëtzebuerg!" or "Looss eis Glässer heben!". The tradition often involves heartfelt toasts and a warm sense of camaraderie.
Raise your glass, say "Salud!", and make eye contact with others as you share your toast. It's a warm and friendly tradition.
Say "Saħħa!" (Health!) — Malta's Kinnie is one of the world's most unusual sodas: bitter orange with aromatic herbs. Pastizzi from a village bakery with cold Cisk is the quintessential Maltese apéro.

Say "Будзьма!" (Budz'ma! — Let's be!) — Belarus's national toast. Draniki (potato pancakes) with sour cream and cold vodka is the quintessential Belarusian apéro — hearty, warming and endlessly shareable.
When making a toast, say "Santé!", "Pour Monaco!" or "Levons nos verres!". The tradition often involves heartfelt toasts and a warm sense of camaraderie.
Raise your glass, say "За здоровье!", and ensure that you maintain eye contact for a proper and heartfelt toast.
When making a toast, say "Skål!", "For Oslo!" or "Løft glassene!". The tradition often involves heartfelt toasts and a warm sense of camaraderie.
Make eye contact while raising your glass and saying "Santé !". In formal settings, glasses are gently clinked together.
Say "Živjeli!" (Long live!) — Montenegro's Njeguški pršut smoked ham, cold Loza brandy and Adriatic sea view at Kotor's bay is one of the Balkans' most spectacular apéro settings.
Raise your glass, say "Na zdraví!", and enjoy the traditional Czech aperitif experience. It's about good health and enjoyment.
When making a toast, say "Priekā!", "Par Rīgu!" or "Celšim glāzes!". The tradition often involves heartfelt toasts and a warm sense of camaraderie.
Raise your glass, say "Salute!", and look into the eyes of the people you're toasting with. It's a sign of sincerity and goodwill.

Say "Cin cin!" — The world's oldest republic sits atop Monte Titano with panoramic views of Italy and the Adriatic. Aperitivo with local Sangiovese wine on one of its three towers is a unique experience.

Say "Živjeli!" (Long live!) — Sarajevo's Ottoman Baščaršija old bazaar and its Austro-Hungarian café culture make for one of Europe's most layered apéro experiences. Ćevapi with cold Sarajevsko is non-negotiable.

Say "Nazdravje!" (To health!) — North Macedonia's Tikveš valley produces excellent Vranec red wine. Ajvar (roasted pepper spread) on crusty bread with cold Skopsko beer is the essential Macedonian apéro.
When making a toast, say "На здраве!", "За София!" or "Да издигнем чашите!". The tradition often involves heartfelt toasts and a warm sense of camaraderie.
When making a toast, say "Skål!", "För Stockholm!" or "Höj glasen!". The tradition often involves heartfelt toasts and a warm sense of camaraderie.

Say "Terviseks!" (To health!) — Tallinn's medieval Old Town taverns serve Vana Tallinn liqueur (a sweet rum-based liqueur) and dark rye bread with sprats. One of the Baltic's most atmospheric apéro settings.
Say "Gëzuar!" (Cheers!/Enjoy!) — Albania's café culture revolves around all-day socialising. Raki (unaged grape spirit) is poured from unlabelled bottles at every opportunity, accompanied by Byrek.
Say "Zum Wohl!" (To your wellbeing!) — Liechtenstein's royal family owns the winery. Sipping Vaduz castle vineyard wine with a Rhine Valley panorama is the apéro of minor European royalty.
Say "Ad multos annos!" (To many years!) — the Vatican's Swiss Guards reportedly enjoy local Roman wine. The Roman aperitivo tradition around St. Peter's Square at sunset is sublimely theatrical.

drinks: Wiener Gemischter Satz (Viennese Field Blend Wine)
aperoInfo.tradition: Raise your glass, say "Prost!" or "Zum Wohl!". It's a friendly and celebratory gesture.

Say "Į sveikatą!" (To health!) — Lithuania's Midus (mead) is one of Europe's oldest alcoholic drinks. Cepelinai (giant potato dumplings shaped like zeppelins) with cold beer is the definitive Lithuanian apéro fuel.
When making a toast, say "Na zdrowie!", "Za Warszawę!" or "Podnieśmy kieliszki!". The tradition often involves heartfelt toasts and a warm sense of camaraderie.
When making a toast, say "Živjeli!", "Za Zagreb!" or "Dignimo čaše!". The tradition often involves heartfelt toasts and a warm sense of camaraderie.
Raise your glass, say "Prost!" or "Zum Wohl!". It's a gesture of appreciation.
Raise your glass, say "Santé!" or "Misaotra!". It's a gesture of good wishes.
Raise your glass, say "Santé!" or "À la vôtre!". It's about well-being and celebration.

Say "Manuia!" (Blessings!) — Samoa's Ava (kava) ceremony is among the Pacific's most formal, reserved for chiefs and important guests. Vailima beer (named after Robert Louis Stevenson's home) is the everyday apéro.

Say "Kia ora!" (Be well/Cheers!) — New Zealand's Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc is the world's most copied wine style. The 5pm "after work drinks" culture is as strong as Australia's, with harbour-view bars in Auckland setting the standard.
Say "Bula!" (Life!/Cheers!) — Fiji's kava ceremony (Yaqona) is the most solemn and communal apéro on earth: drunk from a coconut shell bowl in a circle, clapping before and after each cup.
Say "Biba!" (Cheers/Hooray!) — Guam's Chamorro culture mixes Spanish, Filipino and American influences. Beach BBQs with Kelaguen and cold beer at sunset are the island's defining apéro ritual.
Raise your glass, say "Cheers!" or "To the aloha spirit!". It's about sharing joy and goodwill.
Raise your glass, say "À votre santé!" or "Ia Orana!". It's about well-being and enjoyment.

Say "Gutpela taim!" (Good times in Tok Pisin!) — PNG's SP Lager is a Pacific institution. The country's extraordinary cultural diversity (800+ languages) means every region has its own apéro tradition.
Raise your glass, say "À la vôtre!" or "Manuia!". It's about well-being and enjoyment.
